ddls:
CREATE TABLE "articles" (
  "article_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each article */,
  "headline" TEXT /* Headline of the article */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the article */,
  "topic" TEXT /* Topic of the article (e.g., politics, sports) */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source where the article was published */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Date and time the article was published */,
  "position" REAL /* Position of the article in the news feed */,
  "is_opinion" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the article is an opinion piece */,
  "author" TEXT /* Name of the author(s) of the article */,
  "summary" TEXT /* A brief summary of the article */,
  "word_count" INTEGER /* Number of words in the article */,
  "image_url" TEXT /* URL of the main image associated with the article */,
  "is_premium" INTEGER /* Indicates if the article is premium content */,
  "read_time" REAL /* Estimated time to read the article in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("article_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_articles_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the news source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the news source */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the news source is based */,
  "founding_year" INTEGER /* Year the news source was founded */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the news source */,
  "is_reliable" INTEGER /* Indicates if the source is considered reliable */,
  "rating" REAL /* Average rating of the news source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "topics" (
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each topic */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the topic */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the topic */,
  "parent_topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent topic for hierarchical topic categorization */,
  "icon_url" TEXT /* URL of the icon associated with the topic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "article_topics" (
  "article_id" INTEGER /* ID of the article */,
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the topic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("article_id", "topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date and time the user joined the platform */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user's account is active */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "profile_picture_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile picture */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_articles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "article_id" INTEGER /* ID of the article */,
  "favorite" INTEGER /* Indicates if the article is a favorite of the user */,
  "read" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user has read the article */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* User's rating for the article (1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "article_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "article_sentiments" (
  "article_id" INTEGER /* ID of the article */,
  "sentiment" TEXT /* Sentiment of the article (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "confidence" REAL /* Confidence level of the sentiment analysis */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Numerical score representing the sentiment */,
  "sentiment_model" TEXT /* Name of the sentiment analysis model used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("article_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "entities" (
  "entity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each entity */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the entity */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the entity (e.g., person, organization, location) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the entity */,
  "wikipedia_url" TEXT /* URL of the entity's Wikipedia page */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("entity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "article_entities" (
  "article_id" INTEGER /* ID of the article */,
  "entity_id" INTEGER /* ID of the entity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("article_id", "entity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "article_id" INTEGER /* ID of the article triggering the notification */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Date and time the notification was sent */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., new article, favorite article) */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicates if the notification has been read by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_article_id FOREIGN KEY ("article_id") REFERENCES articles ("article_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "article_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "article_id" INTEGER /* ID of the article being commented on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the comment */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Date and time the comment was made */,
  "upvotes" INTEGER /* Number of upvotes the comment has received */,
  "downvotes" INTEGER /* Number of downvotes the comment has received */,
  "parent_comment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent comment for nested comments */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_article_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_article_comments_article_id FOREIGN KEY ("article_id") REFERENCES articles ("article_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_following" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source being followed */,
  "follow_date" TEXT /* Date and time the user started following the source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "source_topics" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source */,
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the topic being covered */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority of the topic for the source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id", "topic_id")
)
